ExecutionPacket.purpose: Implement the approved module without architectural drift. Follow the contract, preserve semantic markup, run module-local verification.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt: Knowledge graph entry for M-EXAMPLE: path: backend/app/example/ owns: Service logic, API router, data models exports: fn-example, router-example verification-ref: V-M-EXAMPLE

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: backend-core provides settings, DB sessions, security utilities (JWT, password hashing, Fernet)

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: V-M-EXAMPLE MODULE="M-EXAMPLE" PRIORITY="high": test-files: backend/tests/test_example.py module-checks: pytest tests/test_example.py -v scenarios: - kind=success: Primary success path returns expected result - kind=failure: Invalid input rejected with stable error code required-log-markers: [ExampleDomain][function][BLOCK_NAME]

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks — dependency links to other modules

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files — new or modified test files
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks — pytest commands that validate the module

notes.note-1: Workers must never modify docs/*.xml directly — propose deltas to the controller.
notes.note-2: Preserve all START_BLOCK/END_BLOCK anchors when generating code.
Run module-local verification before reporting completion.

exports-added.export-1: fn-do_example — Execute the primary example flow
exports-added.export-2: router-example — FastAPI router with CRUD endpoints

notes.note-1: Only list changes that are real differences in the knowledge graph. Do not list internal helper changes.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Failure path must not emit BLOCK_EXECUTE_FLOW or RETURNED markers.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Error response must include stable error code (not 500 for user errors).

wave-follow-up.item-1: Run integration check for endpoints that depend on M-EXAMPLE

phase-follow-up.item-1: Run broader regression suite before marking phase done
phase-follow-up.item-2: Review recent log markers for semantic consistency

FailurePacket.scenario: VM-EXAMPLE: Primary success path fails on valid input

expected-evidence.assertion-1: Response status 200 with expected result body

observed-evidence.assertion-1: Response status 500, body contains unhandled exception

FailurePacket.root-cause-hypothesis: Missing dependency initialization or invalid state in required service
FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Read MODULE_CONTRACT, inspect BLOCK_VALIDATE_INPUT code, check if required dependency is initialized. Apply smallest safe fix. Rerun module-local verification.

escalation-rules.rule: If the same scenario fails 3 times with different root causes, escalate to $grace-fix for architectural review.
If failure is caused by missing environment (host tools, DB, Redis), mark as ENV_BLOCKED, not TEST_FAILED.
If failure is caused by stale GRACE doc state, run $grace-refresh before attempting fix.
If failure reveals a contract design flaw, do not silently change the contract — propose an update and get approval.
